floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: dead `s32 frames;` is a LUID/pseudo-shift decl; the natural fix is the assign-and-use form (frames = D_800A3858 % 30; centiseconds = frames * 100 / 30) which matches the target's gl

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: plain removal of `s32 frames;` scores 0 (decl was inert - its pseudo never influenced allocation in this small leaf)
frontier.mechanism: unused DECL pseudo is dead from birth; if global.c never tiebroke on its regno the emitted bytes are unchanged
frontier.next_probe: sandbox func_8001CD68 --disable all with line 1479 deleted; 0 = trivial cleanup commit

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: if removal raises the score, the assign-and-use form `s32 frames = D_800A3858 % 30; s32 centiseconds = frames * 100 / 30;` restores the match with full semantic purpose (the judge's own preferred original reading)
frontier.mechanism: same pseudo count as the dead-decl variant (the %-temp gets bound to the frames DECL instead of an anonymous temp), preserving the LUID/regno ordering that seated minutes in $a1 and the reload in $a0
frontier.next_probe: sandbox the assigned form; compare distance vs both current HEAD and the removal variant

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED fallback: decl-placement sweep (frames declared after centiseconds / hoisted to the outer block / centiseconds hoisted) to reproduce the regno shift with a USED variable arrangement
frontier.mechanism: expand_decl ordering controls pseudo numbering feeding global.c's allocation order; several used-variable orderings may hit the same tiebreak
frontier.next_probe: tools/sweep_variants.py over 3-4 decl-order variants if probes 1 and 2 both miss

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Dead local variable `s32 frames;` (src/code6cac.c:1479) is declared inside the inner block, never assigned and never read — GCC DCEs it entirely, so it has zero semantic purpose. It fails test 1 (no observable effect on output) and test 2 (no human programmer writing a frame-count-to-time converter from spec would declare an unused `frames` variable). Either the original source had `frames = D_800A3858 % 30; centiseconds = frames * 100 / 30;` (decomp error — the variable was meant to be used) or it is a LUID-shift declaration inserted to affect register allocation for `centiseconds` (GCC-internals cheat). Both readings fail the standard. Next action: remove `s32 frames;` and re-run sandbox; if score stays 0 the declaration was inert and the fix is trivial (clean up the source); if score rises, the declaration was load-bearing as a LUID cheat and the worker must find a clean C restructuring (e.g., assign `frames = D_800A3858 % 30;` and use it in the centiseconds expression, which would be the natural original form).  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
